title: Driver Acceptance Rate by Pickup Zone

landmines.name: driver-id-as-completion-proxy
landmines.kills: COUNT(driver_id) (or any 'a driver was assigned' proxy) measures the match rate, not the completion rate: driver_id is NULL for no_driver and for riders who cancel before matching, yet non-NULL for matched-then-cancelled_driver rows. Only status='completed' defines acceptance, so the proxy diverges in both value and worst-to-best order.

landmines.name: integer-division-truncation
landmines.kills: completed / total with two integer operands truncates to 0 on every engine; the ratio must be forced to real (1.0 * numerator or CAST) before dividing.

landmines.name: thin-zone-threshold
landmines.kills: Without HAVING COUNT(*) >= 20, thin pickup zones with a handful of requests produce extreme, noise-dominated rates that outrank real zones at the worst-first end of the list.

notes: Purple: conditional aggregation + safe ratio + threshold. The acceptance_rate denominator is total requests per zone (guaranteed >= 20 by HAVING), so there is no divide-by-zero in the reference; the divide-by-zero risk lives in the naive proxies. Fully portable (SUM(CASE), COUNT, ROUND, 1.0* division).
